source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, China/Taiwan fabs, global warming (China's Not Getting Taiwan's Fabs)
summary: SpaceX is the highest risk-adjusted return (Sharpe ratio) asset available in any market, public or private
summary_extended: Baker argues that SpaceX represents the single highest risk-adjusted return (Sharpe ratio) investment available across all public and private markets, making it his highest-conviction idea. His reasoning rests on three pillars: Starlink alone addresses a $1 trillion-plus global telecom market with a product he views as structurally superior to all terrestrial alternatives; SpaceX is the most critical Western defense asset, which he ranks above even Anduril in strategic importance; and the company exhibits 'pro-entropic' characteristics, meaning it becomes more valuable as global disorder increases. Baker also cites long-term optionality by comparing SpaceX's potential trajectory to the British East India Company, implying it could become a dominant commercial and geopolitical force across the solar system. The investment implication is a strong long conviction in SpaceX as a private market holding, with no directly traded public equity proxy he identifies.
detail: Baker calls SpaceX his highest-conviction investment idea on a risk-adjusted basis. Starlink alone addresses a $1T+ telecom market with a uniquely superior product. SpaceX is also the most important Western defense asset (more than Anduril). Long-term optionality includes becoming the 'British East India Company of the solar system.' Pro-entropic: gets more valuable as world disorder increases.
confidence_evidence: Baker explicitly calls it 'the highest Sharpe ratio asset you can buy' — his strongest single investment statement in the transcript
quote_fragment: SpaceX is probably the highest risk-adjusted return available in the markets
